name: Sleep Remedies
description: Extra hours of sleep gained under two drugs for 10 patients (Student 1908).
studyDescription: Ten patients each received two soporific drugs (sleep remedies) in a crossover design. The outcome is the increase in hours of sleep compared to a control (no drug). Each patient serves as their own control, producing 10 paired observations. Originally published by Student (W.S. Gosset) in 1908 — the dataset that motivated the t-test.

variableDescriptions.drug_1: Extra hours of sleep gained (compared to control) under drug 1 (D. hyoscyamine hydrobromide).
variableDescriptions.drug_2: Extra hours of sleep gained (compared to control) under drug 2 (L. hyoscyamine hydrobromide).

sourceDetail: Cushny, A.R. and Peebles, A.R. (1905). The action of optical isomers. Journal of Physiology, 32, 501–510. Reanalyzed by Student (1908). Included in R as datasets::sleep.

context.parameter: mean difference in extra sleep (drug 2 − drug 1)
context.nullClaim: no difference in sleep gained between the two drugs
